description:
A developer has asked you to perform a very quick security-permissions
  sweep of their project directory.

  Existing setup for you to inspect
  ---------------------------------
  1. Project root:   /home/user/my_app
  2. Shell scripts already present:
        /home/user/my_app/deploy.sh
        /home/user/my_app/scripts/cleanup.sh
        /home/user/my_app/scripts/build.sh
     NOTE:
       •  deploy.sh  and  cleanup.sh  have been left world-writable on purpose
          (numeric permission 777).  
       •  build.sh has normal 755 permissions and must NOT appear in the final
          report.
  3.  /home/user/security  does NOT yet exist.

  Your assignment
  ---------------
  1. Create the directory /home/user/security (if it does not already exist).
  2. Identify every “.sh” file inside /home/user/my_app whose *other* users
     have write permission (i.e.  world-writable, permission bit 002 set).
  3. For each offending file, obtain its
        a. absolute path
        b. numeric (octal) permission, **exactly as the `stat -c %a` command
           would output it**  (example: 777)
        c. SHA-256 digest of the file contents, expressed as 64 lowercase
           hexadecimal characters with no leading path or filename.
  4. Produce a single plain-text report located at
        /home/user/security/scan_report.txt

     Output format (strict)
     ----------------------
     • One finding per line.
     • Columns are separated with a single vertical bar “|” character.
     • Column order:  absolute-path  |  octal-permissions  |  sha256-digest
     • Lines **must be sorted in ascending (byte) order by the absolute path**.
       (You can verify by running `sort` on the report; it should remain
       unchanged.)
     • No header row and no trailing blank line.

     Example (illustrative only):
       /abs/path/to/file1.sh|777|<64-hex-chars>
       /abs/path/to/file2.sh|775|<64-hex-chars>

  5. Save the report and make no other file modifications.  The developer
     will examine only the contents of /home/user/security/scan_report.txt to
     confirm completion.

  Success criteria
  ----------------
  • /home/user/security exists.
  • /home/user/security/scan_report.txt exists.
  • The file contains exactly the two lines corresponding to the world-
    writable scripts listed above, in the specified format and order, and
    nothing else.

  This task should require no more than three or four common shell commands
  (mkdir, find/stat, sha256sum, sort, redirects, etc.).
truth:
<!--
      AUTOMATED-TEST GROUND TRUTH
      ---------------------------
      The files listed here already exist when the container is started.
  -->
  <initial_state>
    <file path="/home/user/my_app/deploy.sh" perms="777"><![CDATA[]]></file>
    <file path="/home/user/my_app/scripts/cleanup.sh" perms="777"><![CDATA[]]></file>
    <file path="/home/user/my_app/scripts/build.sh" perms="755"><![CDATA[
#!/bin/bash
echo "Building project"
]]></file>
  </initial_state>

  <!-- Expected final artefact created by the agent -->
  <expected_output file="/home/user/security/scan_report.txt"><![CDATA[
/home/user/my_app/deploy.sh|777|e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
/home/user/my_app/scripts/cleanup.sh|777|e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
]]></expected_output>
